name: Humanin
subtitle: Mitochondria-Derived Peptide | HN
overview: Humanin was the first mitochondria-derived peptide (MDP) discovered, originally cloned from the occipital lobe of an Alzheimer's patient. It is a 24-amino acid peptide encoded from the 16S rRNA region of mitochondrial DNA with potent neuroprotective, cytoprotective, and anti-apoptotic properties. Research shows humanin levels decline with age but remain stable in long-lived species like naked mole-rats, and offspring of centenarians have significantly higher humanin levels.
mechanism: Humanin exerts its effects through multiple mechanisms. It interacts with cell surface receptors including FPRL1 and CNTFR, activating PI3K/AKT signaling pathways that promote cell survival. It blocks pro-apoptotic proteins like Bax, reduces beta-amyloid toxicity in neurons, protects against oxidative stress, and promotes mitochondrial biogenesis. These actions support healthy cellular aging at a fundamental level.
keyBenefits: Potent neuroprotection against Alzheimer's and Parkinson's pathology
Extended lifespan in animal models (C. elegans)
Cytoprotective effects across multiple cell types

items.name: Alzheimer's Disease Protection
items.description: Originally discovered for its ability to protect against amyloid-β toxicity in neuronal cells.

items.name: Parkinson's Disease Support
items.description: Promotes mitochondrial biogenesis via PI3K/AKT signaling in PD models.

items.name: General Neuroprotection
items.description: Broad neuroprotective effects against various neurotoxic insults.

items.name: Lifespan Extension
items.description: Overexpression increases lifespan in C. elegans, dependent on daf-16/Foxo pathway.

items.name: Healthspan Improvement
items.description: HNG analog improves metabolic healthspan parameters in middle-aged mice.

items.name: Anti-Aging
items.description: Higher levels associated with longevity in centenarians and their offspring.

items.name: Cytoprotection
items.description: Protects various cell types from stress-induced damage and death.

items.name: Anti-Apoptosis
items.description: Blocks pro-apoptotic proteins like Bax to prevent programmed cell death.

deliveryMethods.overview: Subcutaneous or intravenous injection. Benefits are slow-building and cellular, typically felt over weeks to months as the peptide supports mitochondria and reduces oxidative stress. The more potent analog HNG (glycine substitution at position 14) is 1000-fold more potent than native humanin.

steps: Clean work area and hands thoroughly
Inject slowly down vial side (not directly onto powder)
Store in refrigerator, protect from light

interactions.notes: Both are mitochondria-derived peptides with complementary longevity and metabolic benefits.
Both target mitochondrial function; may have additive benefits for cellular health.
Different mechanisms; can be combined in longevity protocols.
Both support mitochondrial function and healthy aging.

timeline.effects: Subtle; peptide begins working at cellular level
Improved energy and reduced fatigue may begin
Enhanced cellular resilience and stress response
Cumulative benefits in mitochondrial function and healthspan markers

contraindications: Active cancer (theoretical concern with anti-apoptotic effects)

references.title: The mitochondrial derived peptide humanin is a regulator of lifespan and healthspan
references.keyFindings: Humanin overexpression extends lifespan in C. elegans; HNG treatment improves metabolic healthspan in middle-aged mice; levels higher in centenarian offspring.

references.title: Neuroprotective Action of Humanin and Humanin Analogues
references.keyFindings: Comprehensive review of humanin's neuroprotective effects in Alzheimer's and Parkinson's disease models.

references.title: Mitochondria-derived peptides in aging and healthspan
references.keyFindings: Review of mitochondria-derived peptides including humanin and their roles in aging and metabolic health.

references.title: Original Discovery of Humanin
references.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
references.keyFindings: Hashimoto et al. cloned humanin from Alzheimer's patient brain; demonstrated protection against amyloid-β toxicity.

quickStats.typicalDose: 1-5 mg per injection (0.5-2 mg for HNG analog)
quickStats.frequency: 2-3 times weekly for sustained mitochondrial support
quickStats.storage: Lyophilized: Room temperature. Reconstituted: 2-8°C, protect from light
